facts.text: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <rss xmlns:dc="http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/" xmlns:content="http://purl.org/rss/1.0/modules/content/" version="2.0"> <channel> <title>Latest News from MySQL</title> <link>https://www.mysql.com/news-and-events/news/</link> <description>The latest news from MySQL.</description> <copyright>Copyright 2026, Oracle Corporation and/or its affiliates.</copyright> <item> <title>HeatWave Innovation Rolls on with New Generative AI and Multicloud Capabilities</title> <link>https://www.oracle.com/news/announcement/ocw24-heatwave-innovation-rolls-on-with-new-generative-ai-and-multicloud-capabilities-2024-09-10/</link> <guid>https://www.oracle.com/news/announcement/ocw24-heatwave-innovation-rolls-on-with-new-generative-ai-and-multicloud-capabilities-2024-09-10/</guid> <pubDate>Wed, 18 Sep 2024 11:35:01 +0000</pubDate> <description>Oracle today announced new HeatWave capabilities, including innovations to help organizations more easily and securely take advantage of generative AI both in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) and in Amazon Web Services (AWS).</description> </item> <item> <title>Oracle Announces Industry First In-Database LLMs and an Automated In-Database Vector Store with HeatWave GenAI</title> <link>https://www.oracle.com/middleeast/news/announcement/oracle-announces-in-database-llms-and-automated-in-database-vector-store-with-heatwave-genai-2024-06-26/</link> <guid>https://www.oracle.com/middleeast/news/announcement/oracle-announces-in-database-llms-and-automated-in-database-vector-store-with-heatwave-genai-2024-06-26/</guid> <pubDate>Wed, 26 Jun 2024 16:00:08 +0000</pubDate> <description>Oracle today announced the general availability of HeatWave GenAI, which includes the industry’s first in-database large language models (LLMs), an automated in-database vector store, scale-out vector processing, and the ability to have contextual conversations in natural language informed by unstructured content.</description> </item> <item> <title>Oracle Continues MySQL HeatWave Innovation with Vector Store and New Generative AI Capabilities</title> <link>https://www.oracle.com/news/announcement/ocw-mysql-heatwave-vector-store-generative-ai-2023-09-20/</link> <guid>https://www.oracle.com/news/announcement/ocw-mysql-heatwave-vector-store-generative-ai-2023-09-20/</guid> <pubDate>Wed, 20 Sep 2023 18:15:54 +0000</pubDate> <description>Oracle today announced significant enhancements to MySQL HeatWave, including support for vector store, generative AI, new in-database machine learning features, MySQL Autopilot enhancements, new HeatWave Lakehouse capabilities, support for JavaScript, acceleration of JSON queries, and support for new analytic operators.

The vector store will enable customers to leverage the power of large language models (LLMs) with their proprietary data to get answers that are more accurate than using models which have been trained on public data only.</description> </item> <item> <title>Oracle Announces the General Availability of MySQL HeatWave Lakehouse</title> <link>https://www.oracle.com/news/announcement/oracle-announces-general-availability-mysql-heatwave-lakehouse-2023-07-20/</link> <guid>https://www.oracle.com/news/announcement/oracle-announces-general-availability-mysql-heatwave-lakehouse-2023-07-20/</guid> <pubDate>Thu, 20 Jul 2023 14:17:54 +0000</pubDate> <description>Oracle today announced the general availability of MySQL HeatWave Lakehouse, delivering an industry first by enabling customers to query data in object storage as fast as querying data inside the database.

MySQL HeatWave Lakehouse supports a variety of object store file formats such as CSV, Parquet, and export files from other databases, and can combine object storage file data and MySQL database transactional data together in the same query.</description> </item> <item> <title>Oracle Announces MySQL HeatWave Lakehouse</title> <link>https://www.oracle.com/news/announcement/ocw-oracle-announces-mysql-heatwave-lakehouse-2022-10-18/</link> <guid>https://www.oracle.com/news/announcement/ocw-oracle-announces-mysql-heatwave-lakehouse-2022-10-18/</guid> <pubDate>Wed, 19 Oct 2022 22:00:21 +0000</pubDate> <description>Oracle today announced MySQL HeatWave Lakehouse, enabling customers to process and query hundreds of terabytes of data in object store in a variety of file formats, such as CSV and Parquet, as well as Aurora and Redshift backups.
MySQL HeatWave Lakehouse delivers 17X faster query performance vs.
